name: Burgundy Beef Stew
recipeIngredient: 3.5 lb. beef chuck or shoulder roast (3.5 to 4 lbs.)
4 large stalks of celery, cut into 1/2 inch pieces
1 lb. carrots, peeled and cut into 1/2 inch pieces
1 medium rutabaga, peeled and cut into 1/2 inch cubes
1 1/2 lb. new red potatoes, cut into 3/4 inch pieces (do not peel)
1 lb. onion, peeled and cut into 1 inch pieces
2 large cloves of garlic, finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim and cut the beef into 1-inch cubes, removing as much of the heavy fat as practical.
2. Heat the olive oil in a large skillet.
3. Gradually add the beef to the hot oil.
4. Over high heat, brown the beef until each piece is a rich, dark brown color.
5. Spoon the tomato paste over the browned beef pieces and stir to incorporate.
6. Continue cooking until the tomato paste starts to caramelize.
7. Deglaze the pan with the burgundy wine.
8. In a large stock pot, combine the beef, vegetables, browning sauce, and seasonings.
9. Bring to a boil, reduce heat, and simmer, covered for two hours.
10. Thicken stew with the roux to the desired consistency.
11. Adjust salt and pepper to taste.
12. Note: If beef stock is not available, substitute:
a) Canned beef broth plus 8 beef bouillon cubes
b) 2 quarts water plus 24 beef bouillon cubes
